item.title: Her name is Angeli Rose Gomez
item.description: Her name is Angeli Rose Gomez, a farm supervisor that drove 40 miles to the school.⁣ ⁣ Police then ARRESTED HER. Put her in handcuffs. When she demanded that they intervene and save the kids. ⁣ ⁣ She convinced someone to take the cuffs off.⁣ ⁣ Then ignored their warnings and barriers, marched into the school, and got her two kids.⁣ ⁣ All while police sat on their asses and did NOTHING.
item.dc:creator: mfrybeasley

item.title: Interesting Fact.
item.description: Lavinia Ellen "Vinnie" Ream Hoxie (September 25, 1847 – November 20, 1914) was an American sculptor. Her most famous work is the statue of U.S. President Abraham Lincoln in the U.S. Capitol rotunda.

item.title: Explainer thread about why cops will never EVER endanger themselves. Mind blowing.
item.dc:creator: ednl

item.title: Friday at the Star Wars Celebration in Anaheim.
item.description:
We did this one just for you, my Dudes!
With Dude Vader.
John Brummet did the Force Ghost before anyone else. I was glad to meet him. Outstanding guy.
With John Rhinehart, who plays a great Young Luke. Also a very, very nice guy. I'm lucky to know him.
Force ghost, Bistan, George (F)Lucas, me, Young Luke cosplay.
With the Frog Mom!!!
She had the tank and all! I did not get a pic of it. I has the dumb.
With Darth half-boy and Palps.
Heartburn time, old man!
From young Luke to dead Luke, in order.
With our boy, R2.
A Trooper I met while walking back from the store. He said "Hey, are you Fluke Skywalker? " Yep! Finally, I have people asking that vs. are you. Mark Hamill? That is a good thing for both Mark and me.
Not a cat tax but will a beef cake tax work? Tee hee. A fun shirt I brought to Anaheim. More pix from Disneyland tonight are heading your way, my Dudes. May the Force Be with you!
item.dc:creator: imflukeskywalker

item.description: If this image hurts, disturbs, or makes you uncomfortable. Good, it means you're capable of feeling something, unlike an overwhelming majority of our leadership. Let it help drive you to push forward the changes the U.S desperately needs.

item.title: Disarm and disband the police. They do nothing.
item.description: And worse, they prevented parents from taking action to save their kids and others' kids. Because police exist to enforce order, not to protect anybody. They couldn't let the parents take action because that violates the order they uphold. We would be better off without police. We would be SAFER without police. At least then kids wouldn't call 911 expecting police to show up and help. Even if they did still call 911, firemen might actually do something. Police did the exact same thing in Warren vs DC and the courts ruled that they were legally protected. At least there the victims were adults and not a classroom of elementary school children. The circumstances were incredibly similar. The police were there. The police were lazy cowards. The police did nothing. The police were protected by the courts. As the bodyguards and enforcers of capital and white supremacy, that's all they have to do. > Warren's call was received at Metropolitan Police Department Headquarters at 6:23 am, and was recorded as a burglary-in-progress. At 6:26, a call was dispatched to officers on the street as a "Code 2" assignment, although calls of a crime in progress should be given priority and designated as "Code 1." Four police cruisers responded to the broadcast; three to the Lamont Street address and one to another address to investigate a possible suspect. > Meanwhile, Warren and Taliaferro crawled from their window onto an adjoining roof and waited for the police to arrive. While there, they observed one policeman drive through the alley behind their house and proceed to the front of the residence without stopping, leaning out the window, or getting out of the car to check the back entrance of the house. A second officer apparently knocked on the door in front of the residence, but left when he received no answer. The three officers departed the scene at 6:33 am, five minutes after they arrived. > Warren and Taliaferro crawled back inside their room. They again heard Douglas' continuing screams; again called the police; told the officer that the intruders had entered the home, and requested immediate assistance. Once again, a police officer assured them that help was on the way. This second call was received at 6:42 am and recorded merely as "investigate the trouble;" it was never dispatched to any police officers. > Believing the police might be in the house, Warren and Taliaferro called down to Douglas, thereby alerting Kent to their presence. At knife point, Kent and Morse then forced all three women to accompany them to Kent's apartment. For the next fourteen hours the captive women were raped, robbed, beaten, forced to commit sexual acts upon one another, and made to submit to the sexual demands of Kent and Morse. https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Warren_v._District_of_Columbia Disarm and disband all police.
item.dc:creator: jabbaClickAnimal

item.title: BU’s Prof. Jon Levy on masking up
item.description:
Felt like this deserved a posting
especially given how most people have stopped wearing masks,
which can be fine given the setting,
but absolutely should not make those who are still masked up with feel bad or guilty for doing so.
Because we aren’t.
Regardless of if we’re doing so for ourselves, our loved ones, or just because we want to. Stay safe people, and let others take whatever precautions they feel are necessary. Source: https://twitter.com/jonlevybu Edit: Most viral? I shall revel in the irony of that. :)
item.dc:creator: HomeRunWoman

item.title: What we know about the Uvalde Police training for active shooters
item.description:
In short, they didn't follow through on a god damned thing they were trained to do. Full thread here: https://twitter.com/ByMikeBaker/status/1530357140191186944
https://www.nytimes.com/2022/05/27/us/active-shooting-trainings.html
https://www.nytimes.com/2022/05/26/us/mass-shooting-school-security.html
https://www.nytimes.com/2022/05/27/us/active-shooting-trainings.html
https://www.washingtonpost.com/nation/2022/05/27/uvalde-shooting-police-gunman-shot-olivarez/
https://www.nytimes.com/live/2022/05/27/us/texas-school-shooting
item.dc:creator: drhobotron
